system.description: Blood Fire (Optional): This is caused by an excess of heat in the blood usually introduced from an external source (such as poison or a Wound). Any character whose skin is broken causing a Wound risks succumbing to Blood Fire. When this happens roll 6d10 against Hardiness taking the single lowest result. If the result meets or exceeds the Hardiness then the character develops Blood Fire. It can be treated with a Medicine roll with the physician working to expel the heat and the toxin in the blood through acupuncture. Using Golden Phoenix Pills can bestow a +1d10 to any Medicine roll attempting to cure Blood Fire. This should only be used in gritty campaigns.
system.source: Wandering Heroes of Ogre Gate, Chapter 2: Rules
system.remedy: Golden Phoenix Pills grant +1d10 to Medicine
system.specialRules: Optional: Medicine TN is 7 +1 per Wound; use only in gritty campaigns.
